channel.title: Home on Aditya Ghosh
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Aditya Ghosh

item.title: About
item.description:
I am a Ph.D. student in Statistics at Stanford University, advised by Stefan Wager and Dominik Rothenhäusler.

My research spans causal inference, statistical learning, optimization, policy evaluation in dynamical systems, and distribution shift.

Besides research, I like teaching and mentoring younger students. I previously maintained a blog aimed at helping high school students prepare for Mathematical Olympiads and similar competitions.

In my free time, I enjoy travel, photography, cooking, chess, and movies.

item.title: Publications
item.description:
Non-parametric Causal Inference in Dynamic Thresholding Designs

Aditya Ghosh and Stefan Wager

Submitted · 2025

We study thresholding designs in general dynamic systems, and show that simple reduced-form characterizations remain available for a relevant causal target, namely a dynamic marginal policy effect at the treatment threshold.

arXiv

Which Covariates to Adjust for? Specification-robust Causal Inference in Observational Studies

Aditya Ghosh and Dominik Rothenhäusler

Submitted · 2025

When it is unclear which covariates to adjust for, we provide valid inference for a reweighted population when at least one of the candidate adjustment sets is valid.

item.title: Teaching
item.description:
Stanford University

As instructor

  • Stats 302: Qualifying Exam Workshop (Theoretical Statistics)
    Summer 2025, Summer 2026

  • Stats 302: Qualifying Exam Workshop (Probability)
    Summer 2024

As teaching assistant

  • Stats 361: Causal Inference
    Winter 2025

  • Stats 310B / Math 230B: Theory of Probability II
    Winter 2024

  • Stats 310A / Math 230A: Theory of Probability I
    Autumn 2023

  • Stats 216: Introduction to Statistical Learning
    Winter 2023

  • Stats 209: Introduction to Causal Inference
    Fall 2025
